checklist_items.title: Verify strict input validation for Super App callbacks in Superfluid Host
checklist_items.description: Ensure that the Superfluid Host contract (ID 201) implements comprehensive validation for all parameters received from Super App (Actor ID 2) callbacks via data flow ID 312. This includes checks for data types, ranges, lengths, and consistency to prevent malicious or unexpected inputs from manipulating protocol state, misdirecting funds (Super Tokens, Asset ID 101; Flows, Asset ID 103), or corrupting user data contexts (User Data, Asset ID 107).
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Superfluid Host might not have sufficient validation for all possible inputs from a Super App callback.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strict input validation in the Superfluid Host for calls from Super Apps.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review report of validation logic in Superfluid Host contract; specific unit/integration test cases demonstrating rejection of malformed/malicious inputs from Super App callbacks.

checklist_items.title: Confirm existence and enforcement of Super App security audit process
checklist_items.description: Verify that a formal security audit process is established and consistently enforced for Super Apps (Actor ID 2) before they are registered or allowed to interact significantly with the Superfluid Host (ID 201). Audits should specifically assess the risk of tampered data/calls affecting protocol assets (Super Tokens, Asset ID 101; Flows, Asset ID 103; User Data, Asset ID 107).
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Super App's code can be designed to send malicious or unexpected parameters to the Superfluid Host.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Conduct thorough security audits of Super Apps.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Documented Super App security audit policy and procedure; records of completed audits for a sample of registered Super Apps; evidence of process enforcement (e.g., gating registration on audit completion).

checklist_items.title: Validate effectiveness of permissioned Super App registration mechanism
checklist_items.description: Ensure that the Superfluid Host contract (ID 201), or an associated governance mechanism, implements and enforces a robust permissioned registration process for Super Apps (Actor ID 2). This process must include vetting to prevent malicious or high-risk Super Apps from gaining callback rights that could compromise protocol integrity or assets (Super Tokens, Asset ID 101; Flows, Asset ID 103; User Data, Asset ID 107).
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Super App's code can be designed to send malicious or unexpected parameters to the Superfluid Host.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use permissioned registration for Super Apps.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review of the on-chain registration and permissioning logic; documented Super App vetting criteria and process; audit trail of Super App registration approvals/rejections.

checklist_items.title: Verify Super App Sanitizes or Excludes Sensitive Data from `userData`
checklist_items.description: Confirm through code review and design analysis that the Super App (ID: 2) implements mechanisms to prevent or sanitize sensitive information before including it in the `userData` field (related to Asset ID: 107) of calls to the Superfluid Host Contract (ID: 201). `userData` is public on-chain and mishandling can lead to information disclosure.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Super App might handle or generate sensitive data that it then includes in public calls.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Super App developers should avoid including sensitive data in on-chain calls, especially in userData.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review report for Super App (ID: 2) and/or design documentation demonstrating that sensitive data is not passed in `userData`, or that appropriate sanitization/encryption (if applicable off-chain) is in place.

checklist_items.title: Verify User Documentation Highlights `userData` Publicity
checklist_items.description: Check that user-facing documentation for the Super App (ID: 2) clearly communicates that any data passed via the Super App into fields like `userData` (related to Asset ID: 107) for on-chain transactions will be publicly visible on the blockchain. This ensures users are aware of potential privacy implications.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users of the Super App might not be aware that data passed through the Super App could become public via userData.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Educate users about data privacy implications.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Link to or screenshot of user documentation for Super App (ID: 2) explicitly stating the public nature of `userData` and similar on-chain data.

checklist_items.title: Ensure Super App Callbacks Prevent Reverts in Common Scenarios
checklist_items.description: Review and test Super App callback functions (e.g., `afterAgreementCreated`, `afterAgreementUpdated`, `afterAgreementTerminated`) to confirm they do not revert under expected operational conditions and inputs. This includes checking for logical errors, unmet require conditions, and issues with external calls made within callbacks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Super App's callback logic contains a bug causing it to revert or consume excessive gas.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Thorough testing of Super App callback logic.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review records, unit/integration test reports demonstrating callbacks handle expected scenarios without reverting.

checklist_items.title: Validate Super App Callback Gas Consumption Stays Within Limits
checklist_items.description: Profile and analyze the gas consumption of all Super App callback functions to ensure they execute efficiently and remain well below the gas limits enforced by the Superfluid Host, preventing out-of-gas exceptions and jailing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Super App's callback logic contains a bug causing it to revert or consume excessive gas.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Adherence to gas limits.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Gas profiler reports for all callbacks, stress test results confirming gas usage remains within Superfluid Host limits.

checklist_items.title: Implement Graceful Error Handling in Super App Callbacks
checklist_items.description: Verify that Super App callback functions include robust error handling (e.g., `try/catch` for external calls where appropriate, conditional logic to handle failed operations) to prevent unexpected reverts and ensure predictable behavior even when errors occur.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Super App's callback logic contains a bug causing it to revert or consume excessive gas.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Graceful error handling within callbacks.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code snippets demonstrating graceful error handling patterns (e.g., try-catch, conditional checks), test cases for error condition handling.

checklist_items.title: Audit Super App Design for Jailing Vulnerabilities
checklist_items.description: Review the Super App's architecture and callback logic to identify and eliminate design patterns or specific bugs that could lead to jailing (e.g., unconditional reverts, gas-heavy loops dependent on external state). Focus on preventing conditions that cause reverts or excessive gas usage, in line with understanding the protocol's jailing mechanism.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Super App's callback logic contains a bug causing it to revert or consume excessive gas.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Protocol-level jailing mechanism.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Design documentation outlining jailing avoidance strategies, code audit report identifying and addressing potential jailing triggers based on known jailing conditions.

checklist_items.title: Verify Gas Stipends for Super App Callbacks
checklist_items.description: Ensure that the Superfluid Host Contract enforces a specific, documented gas stipend for callbacks made to Super Apps. This is crucial to prevent gas exhaustion attacks originating from a Super App callback, which could cause the parent transaction to fail.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Superfluid Host does not sufficiently limit the gas that can be consumed by operations triggered from a Super App callback within the parent transaction.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Impose gas stipends for callbacks.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review report or static analysis output confirming the implementation and enforcement of gas stipends for Super App callbacks in the Superfluid Host Contract. Documentation specifying the gas limit values and rationale.

checklist_items.title: Verify Limits on Callback-Induced Operations from Super Apps
checklist_items.description: Check if the Superfluid Host Contract, in addition to gas stipends, implements mechanisms to limit the number or complexity (e.g., types of allowed re-entrant calls, call depth) of subsequent operations a Super App can trigger within its callback context. This provides defense-in-depth against sophisticated gas-related DoS.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Super App can initiate multiple or gas-intensive operations within its callback context.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Limit the number/complexity of operations a Super App can trigger within a callback.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Design documentation and code review findings detailing specific mechanisms (e.g., re-entrancy guards for critical functions, call depth limits, restrictions on specific function calls from callbacks) within the Superfluid Host Contract. Test cases demonstrating these limits.

checklist_items.title: Verify Completion of Security Audits for Core Contracts
checklist_items.description: Confirm that the Superfluid Host Contract (ID 201) and related agreement contracts have undergone recent, comprehensive security audits by reputable third-party firms, specifically looking for vulnerabilities exploitable by Super App callbacks that could lead to elevation of privilege.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A vulnerability exists in the Superfluid Host or agreement contracts that can be triggered by a Super App callback.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Rigorous security audits of Superfluid core contracts.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, auditor credentials, scope of audit, and documented remediation of findings.

checklist_items.title: Confirm Formal Verification of Critical Host Contract Logic
checklist_items.description: Verify that critical sections of the Superfluid Host Contract (ID 201), especially those handling Super App callbacks and interactions with agreement contracts, have undergone formal verification to prove correctness and absence of known vulnerability patterns leading to privilege escalation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A vulnerability exists in the Superfluid Host or agreement contracts that can be triggered by a Super App callback.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Formal verification.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Formal verification reports, tools used, properties verified, and coverage analysis.

checklist_items.title: Review Adherence to Secure Coding Standards in Host Contract
checklist_items.description: Assess the codebase of the Superfluid Host Contract (ID 201) and associated agreement contracts for adherence to established secure smart contract development practices (e.g., checks-effects-interactions, reentrancy guards, safe math, robust input validation for parameters from Super Apps) relevant to Super App callback interactions to prevent exploitation via crafted inputs.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Super App is crafted to send specific inputs to exploit this vulnerability.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Secure coding practices.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review checklists, static analysis tool reports, documented coding standards, and evidence of their application.

checklist_items.title: Verify Super App Registration and Review Process Efficacy
checklist_items.description: Confirm that a robust and consistently applied process is in place for registering and reviewing Super Apps (Actor ID 2 type) before they can interact with the Superfluid Host Contract (ID 201) over the 'Superfluid Host - Super App' trust boundary (ID 404). The review should include checks for potential malicious behavior, vulnerability exploitation patterns, and adherence to security guidelines for Super App development.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Super App is crafted to send specific inputs to exploit this vulnerability.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Permissioned Super App registration and review process.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Documented registration and review procedures, list of approved reviewers, criteria for approval/rejection, and records of past reviews.

checklist_items.title: Verify Correct `msg.sender` Usage for Stake Attribution in TOGA Contract Staking Functions
checklist_items.description: Audit the TOGA smart contract's staking functions (e.g., functions responsible for receiving stakes) to confirm that `msg.sender` is exclusively and correctly used to determine the identity of the staker and assign ownership of the staked TOGA Stake assets (Asset ID 106). Ensure no other mechanisms (e.g., user-provided addresses for ownership, vulnerable delegatecall patterns affecting sender context) can override or misinterpret `msg.sender` for this critical purpose, mitigating the risk of spoofed staking transactions leading to incorrect PIC assignment or loss of funds.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The TOGA contract relies solely on msg.sender for stake ownership, which is standard and generally secure but highlights key compromise risk.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: The TOGA contract must correctly use msg.sender for stake attribution.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review report detailing verification of `msg.sender` usage in all staking-related functions and related state variable updates. Test case results from unit/integration tests covering staking scenarios, specifically focusing on sender identification and stake assignment.

checklist_items.title: Verify User Guidance for Secure Staking Transaction Signing
checklist_items.description: Check that documentation and UI elements for the TOGA staking process explicitly advise PICs (Actor ID: 5) to verify all transaction parameters (e.g., stake amount, target token for PIC role) on a trusted device or hardware wallet before signing staking transactions submitted to the TOGA contract (Component ID: 207).
checklist_items.assumption_ref: An attacker can modify transaction parameters before they are signed by the user (e.g., via a compromised frontend or malware).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Users should verify transaction details on trusted devices/hardware wallets before signing.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshots of UI elements, links to documentation, or user guides that clearly instruct PICs on pre-signing verification of staking parameters.

checklist_items.title: Audit TOGA Contract for Robust Staking Parameter Validation
checklist_items.description: Conduct a thorough code review of the TOGA smart contract (Component ID: 207) to ensure it implements comprehensive server-side input validation for all parameters related to staking transactions (e.g., stake amount, target Super Token). This includes checks for valid data types, ranges, and logical consistency between parameters to prevent unintended staking outcomes such as incorrect stake amounts or PIC assignments for asset TOGA Stake (Asset ID: 106).
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The TOGA contract might not have sufficient server-side validation for all logical parameter combinations if complex staking options exist.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: The TOGA contract should have robust input validation.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract audit report, code review documentation, or specific test cases demonstrating comprehensive input validation for staking functions within the TOGA contract.
checklist_items.owner: Smart Contract Audit Team / Development Team

checklist_items.title: Verify Public Verifiability of PIC Staking Details in TOGA Contract
checklist_items.description: Confirm that the TOGA smart contract (ID: 207) makes PIC staking actions (including PIC address and amount staked) publicly verifiable on the blockchain. This can be through emitted events or readable public state variables. This ensures that a PIC cannot repudiate their staking action, leveraging the blockchain's immutability.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The blockchain provides a reliable and immutable log of transactions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Leverage the inherent non-repudiation properties of the blockchain. All staking actions are publicly verifiable.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract code showing event emission or public state variable for staking details, or a documented procedure to query this information from the deployed contract using a block explorer or script.

checklist_items.title: Verify User Documentation on Staking Data Transparency and Privacy Measures
checklist_items.description: Confirm that official documentation clearly informs Patricians in Charge (PICs) that their staking activities (amount, timing, address) in the TOGA contract are publicly visible on the blockchain. The documentation should also suggest user-side privacy considerations or techniques (e.g., using new addresses) if exposure of financial strategy or capacity is a concern.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The details of staking transactions (who, when, how much) are public on the blockchain.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: This is inherent to public blockchains. Users may employ privacy techniques (e.g., new addresses) if concerned, but this is not a protocol-level mitigation.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Link to or copy of the relevant sections in user guides, FAQs, or risk disclosures for PICs addressing staking data visibility and advice on user-controlled privacy measures.

checklist_items.title: Verify gas efficiency of TOGA staking function
checklist_items.description: Review the TOGA contract's staking function implementation to ensure it is optimized for gas usage, minimizing cost per transaction and susceptibility to gas griefing attacks that could hinder legitimate staking.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The staking function might be susceptible to gas griefing or the network might become congested.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Design gas-efficient staking functions in the TOGA contract.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Gas consumption analysis report for staking operations, code review findings on gas optimization patterns, or relevant sections from a smart contract audit report.

checklist_items.title: Confirm strategy for TOGA staking spam deterrence via network fees
checklist_items.description: Validate that the system design explicitly acknowledges and relies on the underlying blockchain's transaction fee mechanisms to deter spamming of the TOGA staking function. Document if any complementary contract-level controls were considered and the rationale for their inclusion or exclusion.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: An attacker is willing to spend funds (gas fees) to submit many transactions to the TOGA contract.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Rely on network-level transaction fee mechanisms to deter spam.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Design documentation, architectural decision record, or security policy explicitly stating the strategy for spam deterrence and reliance on network fees for the TOGA staking function.

checklist_items.title: Implement monitoring for TOGA staking transaction patterns
checklist_items.description: Establish monitoring and alerting for unusual transaction volumes, high gas prices associated with staking, or repeated failed staking attempts targeting the TOGA contract, which could indicate a DoS or spam attack.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: An attacker is willing to spend funds (gas fees) to submit many transactions to the TOGA contract.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Verify effectiveness and detect circumvention of DoS prevention mechanisms (gas efficiency, network fees) for TOGA staking.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Documentation of monitoring setup, configuration of alerts for anomalous staking activity, or dashboard views showing staking transaction metrics and gas consumption.

checklist_items.title: Verify TOGA contract audit for staking logic DoS vulnerabilities
checklist_items.description: Ensure that the TOGA smart contract (ID: 207) has undergone a thorough security audit, specifically examining its staking function for vulnerabilities like reentrancy, unhandled exceptions, infinite loops, or other Denial of Service vectors as highlighted in threat ID 11.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The TOGA smart contract contains a bug in its staking logic that can be triggered by a staking transaction.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Conduct thorough smart contract audits
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit report for TOGA contract (ID: 207) detailing the review of its staking logic, findings related to DoS vulnerabilities (threat ID 11), and status of remediations.
checklist_items.owner: Smart Contract Audit Team / Security Team

checklist_items.title: Confirm comprehensive testing (including fuzzing) of TOGA staking for DoS resilience
checklist_items.description: Verify that the TOGA contract's (ID: 207) staking function has been subjected to comprehensive test suites, including fuzz testing, specifically targeting Denial of Service scenarios (e.g., gas exhaustion, unexpected reverts due to malformed inputs relevant to threat ID 11). Review test coverage and outcomes.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: This bug leads to staking operations failing or the contract becoming inoperable.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: implement comprehensive test suites including fuzz testing
checklist_items.evidence_required: Test plan, test coverage reports, and fuzz testing results/reports for the TOGA contract's staking function, showing checks for DoS vectors related to threat ID 11.
checklist_items.owner: QA Team / Smart Contract Development Team

checklist_items.title: Evaluate use of formal verification for TOGA staking logic against DoS
checklist_items.description: Assess whether formal verification techniques were applied or appropriately considered for the critical staking logic within the TOGA contract (ID: 207) to prove properties related to Denial of Service prevention or ensure operational continuity, as relevant to threat ID 11.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The TOGA smart contract contains a bug in its staking logic that can be triggered by a staking transaction.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: consider formal verification for critical logic
checklist_items.evidence_required: Documentation of formal verification efforts (if undertaken) for TOGA staking logic, including properties verified against DoS (threat ID 11), or a documented rationale if formal verification was considered but not applied.
checklist_items.owner: Formal Verification Team / Lead Architect / Smart Contract Development Team

checklist_items.title: Verify TOGA PIC Selection Logic for Correctness
checklist_items.description: Review the TOGA smart contract source code to ensure that the logic for determining the Patrician in Charge (PIC) correctly validates stake amounts, stake ownership, and accurately identifies the legitimate PIC based on the defined auction rules. Specifically check for vulnerabilities that could allow becoming PIC with insufficient or illegitimate stakes, or by bypassing comparison logic.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The TOGA contract has a logic flaw in how it determines the current PIC or validates incoming stakes (e.g., stake amount comparison, stake ownership).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Rigorous smart contract audits focusing on auction logic, stake management, and access control.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review report, static analysis results, or formal verification outputs confirming correct PIC selection logic, including stake amount comparison, ownership validation, and auction state transitions.

checklist_items.title: Confirm Comprehensive Audit of TOGA Auction and Stake Logic
checklist_items.description: Verify that a comprehensive security audit of the TOGA smart contract has been completed by a qualified and independent third party. The audit scope must explicitly cover the auction mechanism, stake management functions (including deposits and withdrawals), and access controls governing the Patrician in Charge (PIC) role assignment, specifically addressing potential elevation of privilege vulnerabilities.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The TOGA contract has a logic flaw in how it determines the current PIC or validates incoming stakes (e.g., stake amount comparison, stake ownership).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Rigorous smart contract audits focusing on auction logic, stake management, and access control.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Final audit report from a third-party auditor detailing the scope (covering TOGA auction/stake logic), findings, severity, and remediation status for identified issues related to PIC role assignment.

checklist_items.title: Validate TOGA Test Coverage for Invalid PIC Bidding and Stake Scenarios
checklist_items.description: Review and confirm that the TOGA smart contract's test suite includes comprehensive test cases for Patrician in Charge (PIC) bidding and stake management scenarios. This must cover valid bids, invalid bids (e.g., insufficient stake, staking unowned tokens, re-entrancy on stake/unstake), and attempts to exploit auction logic (e.g., bypassing bid comparison, front-running, manipulating auction timers) to illegitimately gain PIC status.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: An attacker can craft a transaction that exploits this flaw.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Adherence to secure development practices and comprehensive testing.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Test plan, documented test cases, test code, and test execution reports demonstrating coverage for diverse valid, invalid, and adversarial PIC bidding and stake management scenarios.

checklist_items.title: Verify Cryptographic Signature and Token Ownership for Votes
checklist_items.description: Check that the governance voting mechanism enforces strong cryptographic signature verification for each vote, ensuring it originates from the private key associated with a valid SUP token holder's address with sufficient voting power at the time of voting. This must include protections against replay attacks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The voting mechanism relies on credentials (e.g., private keys) that can be stolen or phished.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: enforce strong cryptographic signature verification tied to SUP token ownership at the time of voting
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code audit report verifying signature logic (including replay protection and token ownership checks), design documentation of the voting signature scheme, and results from penetration testing focusing on vote spoofing.

checklist_items.title: Assess and Implement Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for Governance Voting
checklist_items.description: Evaluate the feasibility of and, if viable, implement multi-factor authentication (MFA) for governance voting actions to add a secondary verification layer against compromised primary credentials (e.g., private keys).
checklist_items.assumption_ref: There are no or insufficient secondary checks to validate the authenticity of a vote beyond the primary credential.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement multi-factor authentication for voting where feasible
checklist_items.evidence_required: Feasibility study report for MFA in DAO voting, design specification for the MFA solution (if implemented), and test results or security review of the MFA functionality.

checklist_items.title: Verify DAO Participant Education Program on Credential Security
checklist_items.description: Ensure a comprehensive user education program is in place for Superfluid DAO Participants, focusing on secure management of private keys and awareness of phishing/social engineering tactics that could lead to credential compromise for voting.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The voting mechanism relies on credentials (e.g., private keys) that can be stolen or phished.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: educate users on credential security.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Documentation of educational materials (e.g., guides, articles, warnings), communication plan for security awareness campaigns, and records of training sessions or materials distribution to DAO participants.

checklist_items.title: Verify Input Validation for Governance Proposal Submissions
checklist_items.description: Check that the proposal submission mechanism (e.g., smart contract, interface) implements strict input validation and sanitization to prevent injection of malicious code or misleading information into governance proposals.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The proposal submission interface or smart contract has a vulnerability allowing injection of malicious code or misleading information.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strict input validation and sanitization for proposal submissions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review report of input validation logic in the proposal submission smart contract; Penetration test results focusing on proposal submission vulnerabilities.

checklist_items.title: Confirm Stake/Deposit Requirement for Proposal Submission
checklist_items.description: Verify that a significant stake or monetary deposit is enforced by the smart contract for submitting governance proposals to deter spam and low-effort malicious submissions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A DAO participant's account with proposal rights is compromised.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require a significant stake or deposit for submitting proposals to deter spam/malicious attempts.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract code or parameter review confirming the stake/deposit mechanism and its current value; On-chain data verifying the parameter setting.

checklist_items.title: Assess Multi-Party Review Process for Governance Proposals
checklist_items.description: Ensure a thorough, clearly defined, and consistently followed multi-party review process exists for all governance proposals before they are eligible for a vote, designed to identify and filter out malicious or harmful proposals.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The review process for proposals is insufficient to catch cleverly disguised malicious proposals.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce a thorough, multi-party review process for all proposals before they go to a vote.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Documented governance review process outlining roles, responsibilities, and steps; Records of past proposal reviews demonstrating adherence to the multi-party review process.

checklist_items.title: Verify On-Chain Vote Records and Participant Linkage
checklist_items.description: Confirm that all submitted governance votes are immutably recorded on-chain and are cryptographically linked to the specific DAO participant's identity (e.g., wallet address that is the `msg.sender`).
checklist_items.assumption_ref: If governance actions (votes, proposals) are not securely and irrefutably logged and attributable to the specific DAO participant, they might be able to deny their actions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure all binding governance actions (votes, proposal submissions) are recorded on-chain with clear cryptographic linkage to the participant's identity.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract code review (vote function, event emission), example on-chain vote transactions demonstrating recording and linkage to participant's address.

checklist_items.title: Verify On-Chain Proposal Records and Participant Linkage
checklist_items.description: Confirm that all submitted governance proposals are immutably recorded on-chain and are cryptographically linked to the specific DAO participant's identity (e.g., wallet address that is the `msg.sender`).
checklist_items.assumption_ref: If governance actions (votes, proposals) are not securely and irrefutably logged and attributable to the specific DAO participant, they might be able to deny their actions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure all binding governance actions (votes, proposal submissions) are recorded on-chain with clear cryptographic linkage to the participant's identity.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract code review (proposal submission function, event emission), example on-chain proposal transactions demonstrating recording and linkage to participant's address.

checklist_items.title: Verify Audit Trails and Identity Linkage for Off-Chain Governance Platforms
checklist_items.description: For any off-chain platforms (e.g., discussion forums, signaling tools) that influence governance decisions, verify they maintain strong audit trails of participant actions and that these actions are securely linked to verified participant identities.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Some part of the voting or proposal submission process (e.g., signaling, discussion forums influencing on-chain actions) lacks strong, publicly verifiable, and immutable records linking the action to the participant.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: For any off-chain components, use systems with strong audit trails and digital signatures.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Documentation of off-chain platform features for audit logs and identity verification (e.g., forum software capabilities, signaling platform design using digital signatures), review of how participant accounts are linked to on-chain identities if applicable.

checklist_items.title: Verify Clarity and Explicit Confirmation in Governance Action UI
checklist_items.description: Review the user interface for submitting votes and proposals to ensure it clearly communicates the action being taken, its consequences, and requires explicit user confirmation before submission to blockchain. This helps prevent disputes arising from user misunderstanding or alleged UI manipulation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The on-chain transaction alone is disputed as not fully representing the participant's intent (e.g., claiming UI manipulation or misunderstanding).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure all binding governance actions (votes, proposal submissions) are recorded on-chain with clear cryptographic linkage to the participant's identity. (Note: UX improvements support the goal of non-repudiation by reducing grounds for dispute.)
checklist_items.evidence_required: UI/UX design documents, screenshots/recordings of the voting/proposal submission flow focusing on clarity and confirmation steps, user testing feedback related to understandability.

checklist_items.title: Verify Proposal Submission Fee or Stake Requirement
checklist_items.description: Confirm that a non-trivial financial commitment (fee or stake) is required for submitting governance proposals to deter spam and low-quality submissions. Verify the mechanism for potential refunds or stake return based on predefined criteria (e.g., proposal quality, vote threshold).
checklist_items.assumption_ref: There is a low barrier (cost, stake, reputation) to submitting governance proposals or casting votes.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Introduce a reasonable fee or stake requirement for submitting proposals, which can be returned if the proposal passes certain quality checks or is voted upon.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract code review for fee/stake logic, documented governance parameters detailing fee/stake amounts and refund conditions, test results demonstrating the mechanism.

checklist_items.title: Verify Rate Limiting or Cooldown Periods for Governance Submissions
checklist_items.description: Ensure that effective rate limits or cooldown periods are implemented for governance proposal submissions from a single participant/address. This is to prevent system overload and maintain the usability of the governance process.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The system for reviewing and processing proposals has limited capacity and can be overwhelmed by volume.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement rate limiting or cooldown periods for submissions from a single participant.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract code review (if enforced on-chain), backend service configuration (if enforced off-chain), system design documents specifying limits, and test results verifying the effectiveness of rate limiting/cooldown.

checklist_items.title: Verify Gas Efficiency of Governance Smart Contracts
checklist_items.description: Review key governance smart contract functions (e.g., proposal submission, voting, execution) to confirm they are optimized for gas efficiency. This minimizes the risk of DoS attacks leveraging high gas costs (gas-griefing) and reduces operational costs for participants.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Voting or proposal execution might be gas-intensive, allowing for gas-griefing attacks.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Optimize governance contracts for gas efficiency.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Gas profiler reports for relevant functions, smart contract audit reports with a focus on gas optimization, code review documentation confirming adherence to gas-saving patterns.

checklist_items.title: Verify comprehensive audit and formal verification of governance contract logic
checklist_items.description: Check for evidence of a recent, comprehensive security audit and, if applicable, formal verification reports specifically covering voting power calculation, vote aggregation, and proposal execution logic within the governance smart contracts.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A flaw exists in the governance smart contract's logic related to voting power calculation, vote aggregation, or execution of proposals.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Conduct comprehensive security audits and formal verification of governance contracts.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Security audit reports, formal verification documentation, test plans and results covering identified logic in governance smart contracts.

checklist_items.title: Confirm voting power calculation prevents flash loan attacks
checklist_items.description: Verify that the governance smart contract calculates voting power based on locked or time-weighted stake, and that this mechanism is robust against flash loan-based attacks designed to temporarily inflate voting power.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A flaw exists in the governance smart contract's logic related to voting power calculation, vote aggregation, or execution of proposals.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure voting power is based on locked or time-weighted stake to prevent flash loan attacks.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review artifacts of voting power logic, test cases simulating flash loan scenarios against voting mechanism, audit confirmation of flash loan resistance.

checklist_items.title: Verify effectiveness and non-bypassability of time-locks and veto mechanisms
checklist_items.description: Ensure that implemented time-locks for critical governance changes are appropriately configured (duration, scope of changes) and that any security council veto powers are robust and cannot be bypassed by exploits targeting primary governance logic.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Mechanisms like time-locks or veto powers are insufficient or can also be bypassed by the same exploit.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use time-locks for critical changes and consider a security council with veto power for emergency situations.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration details of time-locks, documented procedures and smart contract code for security council veto, threat modeling report and test cases analyzing potential bypass scenarios.

checklist_items.title: Validate robust testing of governance contract edge cases
checklist_items.description: Review test suites for the governance smart contract to ensure comprehensive coverage of edge cases, particularly in voting power calculation (e.g., zero stake, maximum stake, dust amounts, integer overflows) and proposal execution scenarios (e.g., proposal conflicts, execution failures, re-entrancy vulnerabilities, gas limits).
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A flaw exists in the governance smart contract's logic related to voting power calculation, vote aggregation, or execution of proposals.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust testing for all edge cases in voting power calculation and proposal execution.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Test plans, code coverage reports (e.g., >95% branch coverage for critical functions), specific test cases for identified edge scenarios, and results of test execution.

checklist_items.title: Verify restricted smart contract interaction for Auto-Wrap Service
checklist_items.description: Ensure the Super Token Contract (Component 202) only permits the legitimate Auto-Wrap service (Actor 8) to trigger wrapping operations. This should be enforced via mechanisms like a dedicated, permissioned function callable only by a whitelisted service address or through role-based access control.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Super Token Contract might not be able to distinguish a legitimate service call from a spoofed one if credentials are stolen.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication for the Auto-Wrap service (e.g., dedicated, restricted smart contract interaction, secure key management).
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audited smart contract code demonstrating the access control mechanism (e.g., `onlyOwner`, `onlyRole`, specific address check) for functions related to Auto-Wrap service actions. Configuration details of the whitelisted address or role.

checklist_items.title: Verify Super Token Contract supports revocation/update of Auto-Wrap Service authorization
checklist_items.description: Confirm that the Super Token Contract (Component 202) includes functionality allowing administrators to promptly revoke or update the authorization (e.g., whitelisted address, role) of the Auto-Wrap service (Actor 8) in the event its credentials are compromised.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Auto-Wrap service's transaction origination method (e.g., private key) can be compromised or mimicked.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication for the Auto-Wrap service (e.g., dedicated, restricted smart contract interaction, secure key management).
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audited smart contract code showing functions for managing (e.g., setting, revoking, updating) the authorized Auto-Wrap service address/credentials. Documented procedure for emergency revocation/update.

checklist_items.title: Implement monitoring for anomalous Auto-Wrap service transactions
checklist_items.description: Establish and verify a monitoring system that actively tracks transactions related to the Auto-Wrap service (Behavior 513, involving Actor 8) interacting with the Super Token Contract (Component 202). This system should generate alerts for anomalies such as unusually high transaction volume, unexpected target accounts, or deviations from typical wrapping patterns indicative of spoofing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Auto-Wrap service's transaction origination method (e.g., private key) can be compromised or mimicked.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Monitor for anomalous wrapping activity.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Documentation of the monitoring system configuration (e.g., Forta bots, custom scripts). Evidence of active alerts and defined thresholds for anomalous behavior. Sample alert triggered by a test scenario.

checklist_items.title: Verify Secure Configuration and Access Control for Auto-Wrap Service
checklist_items.description: Check that the Off-chain Automation Service (Actor 8) hosting the Auto-Wrap logic has robust security measures, including restricted access to its configuration, secure secrets management for any API keys or private keys, and a hardened execution environment to prevent unauthorized modifications.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: An attacker gains control over the Auto-Wrap service's execution environment or configuration.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Secure the Auto-Wrap service's infrastructure and code.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Documentation of security configurations, access control policies, audit logs for critical changes to the service.

checklist_items.title: Conduct Security Audit of Auto-Wrap Service Code
checklist_items.description: Perform a thorough security audit of the Auto-Wrap service's codebase (Actor 8) to identify and remediate vulnerabilities that could allow tampering with its logic or parameters before it interacts with the Super Token Contract (Component 202).
checklist_items.assumption_ref: An attacker gains control over the Auto-Wrap service's execution environment or configuration.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Secure the Auto-Wrap service's infrastructure and code.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Security audit report for the Auto-Wrap service code, detailing findings and remediation status.

checklist_items.title: Verify On-Chain Validation of Auto-Wrap Parameters in Super Token Contract
checklist_items.description: Review the Super Token Contract (Component 202) implementation to ensure it performs robust validation of parameters (e.g., amounts, target addresses) for wrap operations initiated by the Auto-Wrap service. This includes checks against user-defined limits, global rate limits, or other consent mechanisms to prevent abuse from a compromised service.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Insufficient validation of wrap parameters by the Super Token Contract against expected behavior or user consent.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement on-chain checks or limits for automated operations if possible.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract code snippets demonstrating validation logic, test cases for these validations, and deployed contract audit report.

checklist_items.title: Implement Anomaly Detection for Auto-Wrap Operations
checklist_items.description: Verify that an anomaly detection system is in place to monitor auto-wrap transactions (Behavior 513) initiated by the Off-chain Automation Service (Actor 8) targeting the Super Token Contract (Component 202). This system should flag unusual volumes, frequencies, or patterns of wrapping that could indicate a compromised service or abuse.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: An attacker gains control over the Auto-Wrap service's execution environment or configuration.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use anomaly detection for wrap operations.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Documentation of the anomaly detection system, alert configurations, and sample alerts or reports demonstrating its effectiveness.
checklist_items.owner: Security Operations Team / Data Analytics Team

checklist_items.title: Verify Data Minimization Policy for Auto-Wrap Service
checklist_items.description: Review and confirm that the Off-chain Automation Service (Actor 8) adheres to a data minimization policy, collecting and retaining only essential user-specific data (e.g., balance thresholds, frequency) required for its auto-wrap functionality.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Auto-Wrap service stores or logs user-specific data related to their wrapping preferences or history.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Minimize collection of sensitive user data.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Documented data minimization policy, data retention schedules, and design documents/code reviews confirming adherence.

checklist_items.title: Confirm Encryption at Rest for Auto-Wrap Service Data
checklist_items.description: Verify that all stored user-specific auto-wrap configurations and activity logs managed by the Off-chain Automation Service (Actor 8) are protected by strong encryption-at-rest mechanisms.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The data storage or access controls for these logs/configurations are insufficient.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Securely store and transmit any necessary configuration data. Implement strict access controls and encryption for service logs and databases.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Encryption configuration details for databases/storage, cryptographic key management procedures, and results of configuration audits.

checklist_items.title: Ensure Secure Transmission of Auto-Wrap Configuration Data
checklist_items.description: Validate that all network communications involving user-specific auto-wrap configuration data by or to the Off-chain Automation Service (Actor 8) are encrypted using industry-standard protocols (e.g., TLS 1.2+).
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The data storage or access controls for these logs/configurations are insufficient.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Securely store and transmit any necessary configuration data.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Network scan reports (e.g., SSL Labs), server/service configuration files demonstrating TLS enforcement, and data flow diagrams indicating secure channels.

checklist_items.title: Validate Access Controls for Auto-Wrap Service Data Stores
checklist_items.description: Audit and confirm that strict access controls (e.g., Role-Based Access Control, principle of least privilege) are implemented and enforced for databases and log storage systems containing sensitive user data for the Off-chain Automation Service (Actor 8).
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The data storage or access controls for these logs/configurations are insufficient.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strict access controls and encryption for service logs and databases.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Access control policies, Identity and Access Management (IAM) role definitions, database user privileges, audit logs of access control configurations and access attempts.

checklist_items.title: Verify Auto-Wrap Service Infrastructure Resilience for Behavior 513
checklist_items.description: Check that the Auto-Wrap service, responsible for Behavior 513 (Auto-Wrap Service Tops Up Super Token Balance), has implemented resilient infrastructure. This includes verifying redundancy setups (e.g., multiple instances, failover mechanisms) and DDoS protection measures to prevent service disruptions and ensure availability.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Auto-Wrap service can be targeted by DoS attacks or suffer infrastructure failures.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement resilient infrastructure for the Auto-Wrap service (redundancy, DDoS protection).
checklist_items.evidence_required: Infrastructure architecture diagrams, load balancer configurations, failover test results, DDoS protection service agreements and reports, disaster recovery plan.

checklist_items.title: Verify User Guidance for Super Token Balance Management and Manual Wrapping
checklist_items.description: Review user-facing documentation, UI elements, and support materials related to Component 202 (Super Token Contract) to ensure users are clearly informed about: 1) The importance of monitoring their Super Token balances, especially if relying on the Auto-Wrap service (Behavior 513). 2) The procedures for manually wrapping tokens if the automated service is unavailable or not used.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users rely significantly or solely on the Auto-Wrap service to maintain their Super Token balances for active streams.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Encourage users to monitor their balances and not solely rely on automation. Provide clear user guidance on manual wrapping.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Copies or links to relevant user documentation, screenshots of UI notifications/tooltips, list of support articles addressing balance monitoring and manual wrapping.

checklist_items.title: Verify User Guidance on Least Privilege ERC20 Approvals for Auto-Wrap Service
checklist_items.description: Check that documentation and UI for the Auto-Wrap service (Actor 8) clearly guide users to grant the minimum necessary ERC20 approvals for their Underlying ERC20 tokens (Asset 102), explaining the risks of unlimited approvals.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The service has been granted significant (potentially unlimited) ERC20 approval by users for their Underlying ERC20 tokens.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Encourage users to grant least privilege ERC20 approvals (specific amounts if possible, though less practical for ongoing service).
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshots of user interface and links to documentation providing guidance on ERC20 approval amounts for the Auto-Wrap service.

checklist_items.title: Audit Security Measures for the Auto-Wrap Service Infrastructure
checklist_items.description: Verify that the Off-chain Automation Service (Actor 8) has implemented and maintains strict security measures (e.g., access controls, vulnerability management, secure key management for its operational wallets) to prevent compromise.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Auto-Wrap service is compromised by an attacker.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strict security measures for the Auto-Wrap service.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Security audit report, documentation of security controls, penetration test results for the Auto-Wrap service infrastructure and operational procedures.

checklist_items.title: Evaluate and Verify Implementation of Constrained ERC20 Approvals for Auto-Wrap
checklist_items.description: Assess whether the Super Token Contract (Component 202) or associated approval mechanisms support, or could support, ERC20 approvals for Asset 102 that are constrained (e.g., rate-limited, time-locked, or tied to specific operations by the Auto-Wrap service). If such mechanisms are implemented, verify their correct functionality and enforcement.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The service has been granted significant (potentially unlimited) ERC20 approval by users for their Underlying ERC20 tokens.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Consider time-locked or rate-limited approvals if feasible.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Design documents evaluating feasibility, smart contract code excerpts demonstrating constrained approval mechanisms, and test cases verifying their correct operation.
checklist_items.owner: Smart Contract Development Team / Security Architect

checklist_items.title: Verify UI for User Review and Revocation of Auto-Wrap Service Approvals
checklist_items.description: Ensure that users have a clear, accessible, and well-documented interface to review their current ERC20 approvals granted to the Auto-Wrap service (Actor 8) for Underlying ERC20 Tokens (Asset 102) and an easy way to revoke or modify them.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The service has been granted significant (potentially unlimited) ERC20 approval by users for their Underlying ERC20 tokens.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Users should regularly review and revoke unnecessary approvals.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshots or video walkthrough of the UI feature for reviewing and revoking ERC20 approvals related to the Auto-Wrap service; links to relevant user documentation.

checklist_items.title: Verify Robust Testing of Auto-Wrap Service Logic
checklist_items.description: Ensure comprehensive test suites cover edge cases, error handling, and configuration parameters for the Auto-Wrap service (Behavior 513) to prevent excessive wrapping bugs affecting Component 202 and Asset 102.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Auto-Wrap service contains a bug or is misconfigured leading to excessive wrapping.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust testing and monitoring for the Auto-Wrap service.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Test plans, code coverage reports, and test execution results for the Auto-Wrap service logic related to Behavior 513.
checklist_items.owner: Off-chain Service Development Team / QA Team

checklist_items.title: Verify Monitoring for Auto-Wrap Service Activity
checklist_items.description: Confirm that monitoring systems are in place to detect abnormal or excessive wrapping activity by the Auto-Wrap service (Behavior 513), including alerts for high-frequency or large-volume operations potentially impacting Component 202 and Asset 102.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users may not immediately notice the excessive wrapping activity.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust testing and monitoring for the Auto-Wrap service.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Monitoring dashboard configurations, alert definitions, and documented incident response procedures for anomalies in Behavior 513.

checklist_items.title: Verify Implementation of Circuit Breakers/Rate Limits for Auto-Wrap Service
checklist_items.description: Check that the Auto-Wrap service (Behavior 513) incorporates circuit breakers or configurable rate limits to halt or throttle automated wrapping operations if predefined thresholds are exceeded, protecting Component 202 and Asset 102.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Auto-Wrap service contains a bug or is misconfigured leading to excessive wrapping.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Include circuit breakers or rate limits for automated actions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review of circuit breaker/rate limit logic in the Auto-Wrap service, configuration files defining thresholds, and test cases verifying these mechanisms for Behavior 513.

checklist_items.title: Review ERC20 Approval Strategy for Auto-Wrap on Super Token Contract
checklist_items.description: Assess if the ERC20 approval mechanism on the Super Token Contract (Component 202), as utilized by the Auto-Wrap service (Behavior 513), balances functionality with risk, potentially through guidance or limits, to protect Underlying ERC20 Tokens (Asset 102).
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The service has sufficient ERC20 approval to perform these excessive wraps.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust testing and monitoring for the Auto-Wrap service. Include circuit breakers or rate limits for automated actions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Documentation of the approval strategy, code review of relevant Super Token Contract approval functions and Auto-Wrap service interaction logic for Behavior 513.
checklist_items.owner: Smart Contract Team / Off-chain Service Development Team

checklist_items.title: Verify User Notification System for Auto-Wrap Activity
checklist_items.description: Ensure users receive clear and timely notifications for significant auto-wrap events related to Behavior 513, such as service activation, successful wraps, or changes in settings, to help them monitor activity concerning their Asset 102.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users may not immediately notice the excessive wrapping activity.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Provide users with clear notifications about auto-wrap activity and easy ways to disable the service or adjust settings.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshots of notification content, documentation of notification triggers, and user testing feedback on notification clarity for Behavior 513.

checklist_items.title: Verify User Controls for Auto-Wrap Service Management
checklist_items.description: Confirm that users have accessible and easy-to-use controls to manage the Auto-Wrap service (Behavior 513), including enabling/disabling, adjusting parameters, and revoking permissions related to their Asset 102 and its interaction with Component 202.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users may not immediately notice the excessive wrapping activity.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Provide users with clear notifications about auto-wrap activity and easy ways to disable the service or adjust settings.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshots or walkthrough of the user interface for managing the Auto-Wrap service (Behavior 513), and relevant user documentation.

checklist_items.title: Verify `msg.sender` Authentication for Flow Operator Actions in CFAv1Forwarder
checklist_items.description: Review the CFAv1Forwarder contract (ID: 210) to ensure that all functions callable by a Flow Operator (e.g., for creating, updating, or deleting flows) rigorously use `msg.sender` as the sole source for authenticating the operator. This verifies the fundamental mechanism preventing unauthorized execution by actors other than the declared operator.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The CFAv1Forwarder contract authenticates the Flow Operator via msg.sender.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Flow Operators must secure their private keys. Original senders should monitor operator activity and be able to revoke permissions promptly.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code snippets from CFAv1Forwarder showing `msg.sender` checks for operator actions and references to test cases verifying this logic.

checklist_items.title: Verify CFAv1Forwarder's Adherence to Revoked Flow Operator Permissions
checklist_items.description: Confirm that the CFAv1Forwarder contract (ID: 210), prior to executing flow management actions for a Flow Operator, correctly re-validates the operator's current permissions against the authoritative source (e.g., ACL contract, token allowances). This ensures that actions are blocked if the original sender has revoked the operator's permissions, mitigating risks from compromised operator keys.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Flow Operator's private key can be stolen or misused.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Original senders should monitor operator activity and be able to revoke permissions promptly.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract code demonstrating checks against an ACL or allowance mechanism that can be updated by the original sender. Test cases for scenarios where permissions are revoked.

checklist_items.title: Confirm Event Logging for Sender Monitoring of Flow Operator Actions
checklist_items.description: Verify that the CFAv1Forwarder contract (ID: 210) emits detailed events for all flow management actions (creations, updates, deletions) performed by Flow Operators. These events are crucial for enabling original senders to monitor operator activity and detect potential misuse or compromise.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Flow Operator's private key can be stolen or misused.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Original senders should monitor operator activity and be able to revoke permissions promptly.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Documentation of events emitted by CFAv1Forwarder for operator actions. Sample queries or interface views demonstrating how a sender can access these activity logs.

checklist_items.title: Verify Flow Operator's Pre-Signature Transaction Review Process
checklist_items.description: Confirm that Flow Operators are mandated and trained to use trusted displays (e.g., hardware wallet screens) to meticulously verify all critical parameters (receiver address, flowRate, token address) of transactions targeting the CFAv1Forwarder Contract before signing. This addresses the risk of parameter alteration in a compromised operator environment.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: An attacker can modify transaction parameters before they are signed by the Flow Operator (e.g., via malware on the operator's device).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Flow Operators should use secure environments, verify transaction details on hardware wallets before signing, and use trusted RPC endpoints.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Documented operational security guidelines for Flow Operators; Training completion records; Sample of hardware wallet transaction verification steps.

checklist_items.title: Ensure Clear and Unambiguous Parameter Display in Client Interfaces for CFAv1Forwarder Interactions
checklist_items.description: Review client applications (dApps) that interact with the CFAv1Forwarder Contract to confirm they clearly and accurately present all flow management parameters (token, receiver, flowRate, userData) to the Flow Operator before transaction signing. The display should minimize ambiguity and facilitate easy verification against intended values.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Flow Operator might use a compromised frontend or RPC that alters transaction data.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Client-side validation and clear presentation of parameters are important.
checklist_items.evidence_required: UI/UX design specifications for flow management; Screenshots/recordings of the parameter display in representative client applications; Usability test reports focusing on parameter verification.

checklist_items.title: Verify Client-Side Input Validation for CFAv1Forwarder Flow Parameters
checklist_items.description: Audit client applications to ensure robust input validation (e.g., address checksums, sane numeric ranges for flowRate, valid token contract addresses) is performed on all parameters intended for the CFAv1Forwarder Contract before transaction construction. This serves as a preliminary defense against malformed or potentially malicious inputs.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Flow Operator might use a compromised frontend or RPC that alters transaction data.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Client-side validation and clear presentation of parameters are important.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review report highlighting input validation logic for flow parameters; Test plan and results for client-side validation routines.

checklist_items.title: Promote Usage of Trusted RPC Endpoints for CFAv1Forwarder Transactions
checklist_items.description: Verify that client applications interacting with CFAv1Forwarder Contract guide users towards, or default to, trusted and secure RPC endpoints. Assess if there are warnings or preventions against using potentially compromised or malicious RPCs that could tamper with transaction data in transit to the blockchain node.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Flow Operator might use a compromised frontend or RPC that alters transaction data.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Flow Operators should use secure environments, verify transaction details on hardware wallets before signing, and use trusted RPC endpoints.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Documentation guiding users on RPC selection; Default RPC configurations in client applications; Screenshots of warnings or RPC selection mechanisms.

checklist_items.title: Confirm On-Chain Logging and Signature Verification for Flow Operator Actions via CFAv1Forwarder
checklist_items.description: Verify that all flow management operations (create, update, delete) submitted by a Flow Operator through the CFAv1Forwarder contract result in cryptographically signed transactions and emit unambiguous events on the blockchain. Ensure these records are sufficient to serve as non-repudiable proof of the operator's actions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Blockchain records and cryptographic signatures are considered valid proof of action.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Leverage blockchain immutability and transaction signature verification as evidence of actions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Sample transaction hashes from the CFAv1Forwarder contract demonstrating signed flow operations by a Flow Operator; review of contract event emissions related to these operations to confirm clarity and completeness for audit.

checklist_items.title: Verify User Notification of Public Data Exposure for Stream Operations
checklist_items.description: Ensure that users interacting with the CFAv1Forwarder Contract are clearly informed (e.g., via UI messages, tooltips, or documentation accessible during the transaction process) that all stream parameters (token, original sender, receiver, flowRate, userData) will be publicly recorded on the blockchain.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The details of stream management actions (parties involved, amounts, timing) are considered sensitive by some users.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Users requiring privacy should be aware of the public nature of blockchain transactions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshots of UI notifications, excerpts from user guides, or recordings of the user flow demonstrating the information provided to the user before transaction submission.

checklist_items.title: Verify Documentation on Privacy Limitations and Alternatives
checklist_items.description: Check that official Superfluid documentation clearly states the public nature of stream data transacted via CFAv1Forwarder and, where appropriate, guides users towards external privacy-enhancing solutions if transaction confidentiality is a primary concern.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The operations occur on a public, transparent blockchain.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Privacy-enhancing technologies could be used at a different layer if confidentiality is paramount (not a Superfluid feature).
checklist_items.evidence_required: Links to specific documentation pages or sections addressing data privacy for streams and guidance on external privacy solutions.

checklist_items.title: Verify Guidance on Minimal Operator Permissions and Allowances for CFAv1Forwarder Users
checklist_items.description: Check that documentation and user interfaces (e.g., dApp frontend) clearly guide original senders to grant only the minimum necessary permissions and flow rate allowances to Flow Operators interacting with the CFAv1Forwarder contract, to limit potential for abuse leading to increased gas costs or network impact.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The original sender granted extensive permissions or a high flow rate allowance.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Original senders should grant minimal necessary permissions and flow rate allowances.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshots of relevant documentation sections and UI elements demonstrating guidance on least privilege for Flow Operator permissions and allowances.

checklist_items.title: Confirm Monitoring for Anomalous Flow Operator Activity via CFAv1Forwarder
checklist_items.description: Verify that a monitoring system is implemented and operational to detect and alert on patterns of Flow Operator activity via the CFAv1Forwarder contract that may indicate abuse, such as unusually high transaction frequency or gas consumption attributable to a single operator.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Flow Operator can generate enough transactions to significantly impact gas prices or the original sender's ability to transact.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement monitoring for unusual operator activity.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Documentation of the monitoring setup, configuration of alert thresholds for operator activity, and example alerts or logs showing detection capabilities.

checklist_items.title: Assess CFAv1Forwarder for Operator-Level Gas Limits or Throttling Mechanisms
checklist_items.description: Review the CFAv1Forwarder smart contract and protocol design to assess the feasibility and impact of implementing operator-specific gas limits or transaction throttling mechanisms. This is to prevent a single malicious or compromised Flow Operator from causing excessive gas costs or network congestion.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Flow Operator can generate enough transactions to significantly impact gas prices or the original sender's ability to transact.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: The protocol could implement gas limits or transaction throttling at the operator level if this becomes a widespread issue.
checklist_items.evidence_required: A documented technical assessment or feasibility study on implementing operator-level gas/transaction controls for the CFAv1Forwarder contract.

checklist_items.title: Verify CFAv1Forwarder External Security Audit Completion
checklist_items.description: Confirm that the CFAv1Forwarder contract (ID: 210) has undergone at least one comprehensive security audit by a reputable third-party. Review the audit report for identified vulnerabilities, particularly those that could lead to Denial of Service (e.g., reentrancy, gas issues), and verify their remediation or accepted risk.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The CFAv1Forwarder contract contains an exploitable bug (e.g., reentrancy, uncontrolled gas consumption).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Rigorous smart contract auditing
checklist_items.evidence_required: Final audit report(s), list of findings, and documentation of remediation or risk acceptance for each finding.
checklist_items.owner: Security Team / Smart Contract Development Lead

checklist_items.title: Assess Formal Verification Application for CFAv1Forwarder
checklist_items.description: Evaluate if formal verification techniques were applied to critical components or properties of the CFAv1Forwarder contract (ID: 210) to mathematically prove correctness against specific Denial of Service vectors like reentrancy or unbounded loops.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The CFAv1Forwarder contract contains an exploitable bug (e.g., reentrancy, uncontrolled gas consumption).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: formal verification
checklist_items.evidence_required: Documentation of formal verification scope, tools used, properties verified, and results/reports.
checklist_items.owner: Smart Contract Development Lead / R&D Team

checklist_items.title: Verify Reentrancy Protection Mechanisms in CFAv1Forwarder
checklist_items.description: Conduct a code review of the CFAv1Forwarder contract (ID: 210) to ensure robust reentrancy protection (e.g., Checks-Effects-Interactions pattern, OpenZeppelin ReentrancyGuard) is implemented in all functions involving external calls or state changes that could be exploited for Denial of Service.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The CFAv1Forwarder contract contains an exploitable bug (e.g., reentrancy, uncontrolled gas consumption).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: adherence to secure development practices for the CFAv1Forwarder contract.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review report confirming reentrancy guard implementations, or static analysis tool reports highlighting relevant sections.

checklist_items.title: Analyze CFAv1Forwarder for Uncontrolled Gas Consumption Risks
checklist_items.description: Review the CFAv1Forwarder contract (ID: 210) code for patterns that could lead to uncontrolled gas consumption (e.g., unbounded loops, operations on large arrays/mappings subject to attacker influence, gas-intensive external calls without limits). This is to prevent Denial of Service by making transactions excessively costly or impossible.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The CFAv1Forwarder contract contains an exploitable bug (e.g., reentrancy, uncontrolled gas consumption).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: adherence to secure development practices for the CFAv1Forwarder contract.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review documentation on gas optimization and safety. Gas usage test results for critical functions under various load scenarios.

checklist_items.title: Verify CFAv1Forwarder correctly enforces flow rate allowance against Operator permissions
checklist_items.description: Test and review the CFAv1Forwarder contract, along with the Superfluid Host and Constant Flow Agreement (CFA) contracts it interacts with, to ensure they strictly prevent a Flow Operator from creating or updating a flow where the `flowRate` exceeds the operator's granted allowance for the specific sender and token. This check must include various scenarios, edge cases, and ensure that allowance checks are not bypassable.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A Flow Operator attempts to perform an action (e.g., set flowRate higher than allowance, delete flow without delete permission) for which they are not authorized.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Comprehensive and robust permission and allowance validation logic within the Superfluid Host and CFA contracts.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Documented test cases (including inputs and expected outcomes for exceeding allowance) and their results. Code review records or static analysis reports confirming the presence and correctness of allowance checks in CFAv1Forwarder, Superfluid Host, and CFA contracts. Relevant sections of security audit reports.

checklist_items.title: Verify CFAv1Forwarder enforces granular flow operation permissions (create/update/delete)
checklist_items.description: Test and review the CFAv1Forwarder contract, and the underlying Superfluid Host and CFA contracts, to ensure a Flow Operator is strictly restricted to only the specific actions (e.g., create, update, delete flow) for which they have been explicitly granted permission by the token holder. Verify that attempts to perform unauthorized action types (e.g., deleting a flow when only create/update permission is granted) are reliably rejected.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A Flow Operator attempts to perform an action (e.g., set flowRate higher than allowance, delete flow without delete permission) for which they are not authorized.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Comprehensive and robust permission and allowance validation logic within the Superfluid Host and CFA contracts.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Documented test cases demonstrating rejection of unauthorized action types based on granular permissions. Code review records or static analysis reports confirming action-specific permission checks. Relevant sections of security audit reports.

checklist_items.title: Confirm comprehensive audit and formal verification of access control logic
checklist_items.description: Verify that the permission and allowance enforcement logic within the CFAv1Forwarder, Superfluid Host, and Constant Flow Agreement (CFA) contracts has undergone thorough, independent security audits and, where applicable, formal verification. Ensure these reviews specifically targeted privilege escalation vulnerabilities related to Flow Operator permissions and allowances, and that all critical findings have been addressed.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A vulnerability exists in the permission enforcement logic of the CFAv1Forwarder, Superfluid Host, or the Constant Flow Agreement contract.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Thorough audits and formal verification of these critical checks.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Final security audit reports from reputable third-party auditors. Formal verification reports (if applicable). Documentation detailing the scope of these reviews and how any identified vulnerabilities related to access control were remediated.

checklist_items.title: Verify Sentinel EOA Key Management Policy
checklist_items.description: Ensure a documented and enforced key management policy is in place for Sentinel EOA private keys, covering secure generation, storage, access control, rotation, and revocation procedures to prevent unauthorized use when interacting with the Superfluid Host Contract.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Sentinel's EOA private key can be stolen or otherwise compromised.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust key management, operational security practices, and potentially multi-sig or hardware wallets for Sentinel EOA keys, especially for PICs.
